column1: Isn't a million, five a little steep, even if the suit IS imported?

column1: There's nothing else here you can afford.

column1: You select a reasonably tight Spandex job in blue. Although it's cut a little tight, perhaps that may work to your advantage on the ship. In fact, this may be the beginning of a whole new image for you, Larry!

column1: Presently, you don't owe any money.
column2: 目前你沒欠任何錢。

column2: 「很抱歉，」黑髮的義大利美女說，「我只能在櫃檯前面收購物款！」

column1: You don't have $1,500,000!
column2: 你沒有一百五十萬美元！

column1: You're sure to endear yourself instantly to her with that pseudo-bi-lingualism.

column1: (Ouch.)

column1: She successfully ignores you.

column1: Excuse me, miss, you say hesitantly. "Do you have change for a million?"
column2: 「不好意思，小姐，」你猶豫地說。「妳能找開一百萬嗎？」

column1: Bella bueno, she smiles, "but, of course. But, is that trivial, little, clearance item all you are going to purchase today?"

column1: Yes, ma'am, you reply, handing over your lottery winnings. "I've only got a million on me!"
column2: 「是的，女士，」你回答，遞出你的樂透獎金。「我身上就只帶了一百萬！」

column1: All right, that will be $106,500.00 including tax, your change is $893,500.00.

column1: That's one hundred dollars...

column1: two hundred dollars...

column1: four hundred dollars...

column1: STOP! Don't count any more, you shout, "I don't have THAT long before my ship leaves!!"

column1: Very well, sir, she concludes. "%s"
column2: 「好的，先生，」她做出結論。「%s」

column2: 從藥師櫃檯上的告示看來，你猜藥師外出了。只剩一個店員在顧店，而且看起來不太靈光。

column1: You take a bottle of sunscreen.

column1: Put it back where you got it.

column1: Hey, buddy! you say, "Have you heard I'm now incredibly wealthy?"

column1: Gosh, he replies, "I'm so impressed."

column1: Nope, he responds.

column1: Do you have change for a million-dollar bill?

column1: Do you have change for a hundred?

column1: But all I have is a dollar.

column1: But I have no money, you whine. "Could you find it in your heart to let me have a little bottle of sunscreen to protect my skin from the ozone-depleted atmosphere?"
column2: 「可是我沒錢，」你哀求道。「你能不能發發慈悲，給我一小瓶防曬乳，讓我的皮膚不被臭氧層破洞的大氣傷害？」

column1: Yeah, sure, he says, "do I look like they'd trust me with a million in change?"
column2: 「喔，是喔，」他說，「你看我像是他們會放心讓我準備一百萬找零的人嗎？」

column1: But, of course, my good man, you tell him, suddenly putting on airs, "have a hundred; in fact, have two. Money means nothing to me!"
column2: 「這是當然的，我的好夥伴，」你突然擺起架子對他說，「拿一百元吧；不，乾脆拿兩百。錢對我來說根本不算什麼！」

column1: No way, dude!

column1: Like, thanks, dude, he tells you, "now let me go back to sleep."

column1: Looks like you're out of luck here, Larry.

column1: You find women sexy!

column1: Do you have change for a million-dollar bill? you ask the barber.
column2: 「你能找開一張一百萬元的鈔票嗎？」你問理髮師。

column1: Get lost, replies the barber, "I don't appreciate practical jokes."
column2: 「滾開，」理髮師回答，「我不欣賞這種惡作劇。」

column1: You'd like to get a haircut, but you just don't have enough money!
column2: 你想剪個頭髮，可是你的錢就是不夠！

column1: In fact, I'd appreciate it if you'd leave now before I have to call the Hair Police!
column2: 「而且，我會很感激你現在就離開，免得我得叫髮警來抓你！」

column1: If you want me to work on that problem head of yours, you'd better sit down in the chair.
column2: 「如果你要我處理你那顆問題頭，最好乖乖坐到椅子上。」

column1: Good day, monsieur, says the barber. "May I help you?"
column2: 「日安，先生，」理髮師說。「需要什麼服務嗎？」

column1: How about a quick styling? you ask.
column2: 「快速造型一下如何？」你問道。

column1: Oui, monsieur, but of course, replies the barber, "allow me to correct that receding hairline of yours with my patented, special, proprietary technique... macrobiotic styling reweaving!"
column2: 「Oui，先生，那是當然，」理髮師回答，「讓我用我的獨門專利特殊技術，幫你矯正那條後退的髮線……長壽有機造型編織法！」

column1: Here, says the barber, "take a look in this mirror and remember what you look like. When I'm done, you won't be able to recognize your own hairline! Why, you'll be a different man!"
column2: 「來，」理髮師說，「先照照這面鏡子，記住你現在的樣子。等我弄完，你連自己的髮線都認不出來！哎呀，你會脫胎換骨變成另一個人！」

column1: You think to yourself, "Yeah, this is exactly what I need... a new look. I'll make Bachelorette Barbie change her tune!"
column2: 你心想：「沒錯，這正是我需要的……一個全新造型。看我怎麼讓單身女郎芭比對我改觀！」

column1: Ok, go for it! you tell the barber.
column2: 「好，動手吧！」你對理髮師說。

column1: Allow me to begin with a thorough cleansing to rid your hair and scalp of any possible pollutants, says the barber. "I assure you, I use only the trendiest products, all organically grown and available only in undersized, overpriced, biodegradable bottles!"
column2: 「讓我先徹底清潔一下，去除你頭髮和頭皮上可能殘留的所有污染物，」理髮師說。「我保證，我用的全是最時髦的產品，全部有機栽種，而且只裝在小小一瓶、貴得要命、可生物分解的瓶子裡！」

column1: You wonder to yourself, "What will I look like when he's done?"
column2: 你心想：「等他弄完，我會變成什麼樣子呢？」

column1: Will I be handsome?
column2: 「我會變帥嗎？」

column1: Will I ever find true love?
column2: 「我會找到真愛嗎？」

column1: You certainly have interesting daydreams! says the barber.
column2: 「你的白日夢還真有意思！」理髮師說。

column1: Hey, Brutus! Get outta here!!
column2: 嘿，布魯特斯！給我滾出去！！

column1: Your hair is clean and conditioned, says the barber, "now for the special styling."
column2: 「你的頭髮已經洗淨潤過了，」理髮師說，「接下來進行特殊造型。」

column1: However, in the future, may I recommend 10w40 and no more than 3,000 miles between oil changes!
column2: 「不過，以後我建議你用10W40機油，而且每三千英里就該換一次油！」

column1: All done, says the barber. "Take a look in this mirror... what do you think?"
column2: 「全部完成了，」理髮師說。「照照這面鏡子……你覺得怎麼樣？」

column1: Oh, well. You can't judge a book by its cover!
column2: 唉，算了。人不可貌相嘛！

column1: That'll be fine, you tell the barber.
column2: 「這樣就可以了，」你對理髮師說。

column1: Say, you ask him, "exactly how could I know I was getting a macrobiotic styling?"
column2: 「話說，」你問他，「我要怎麼確定自己做的真的是長壽有機造型？」

column1: By the price!
column2: 「看價錢就知道！」

column1: That'll be a hundred bucks, says the barber.

column1: Thanks, I guess, you say, flipping the barber one of your C-notes.
